page: Microsoft Azure D32lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dldsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor using the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, which provides a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is supported by a bundled 1890 GB local disk, and the server is Premium Storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong performance in specific compute tasks. It achieves top-tier status in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, alongside strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark CPU physics scores. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks fall within the average range, as do standard stress-ng CPU benchmarks. The processor includes Advanced Matrix Extensions and Advanced Vector Extensions, which contribute to strong prompt processing and top-tier text generation speeds during small-model LLM inference.

The primary tradeoff of the D32lds_v7 is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio, which makes it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or large-scale caching layers. However, this resource density provides qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive RAM footprints. The combination of dedicated Intel Xeon cores, local storage, and strong vector instruction support makes this server well-suited for web serving, batch processing, compression tasks, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D32lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it features a low-memory configuration of 64.0 GB RAM (2.0 GB per vCPU) and includes a bundled 1890 GB local disk. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression and small-model LLM text generation, alongside strong web serving throughput and PassMark CPU scores. While its low memory-to-vCPU ratio presents a tradeoff for memory-bound applications, it offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-focused tasks that do not require massive RAM footprints. This server is highly suited for web serving, batch processing, data compression, and moderate machine learning inference workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 1890 GB local disk, delivering top-tier performance in data compression.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32lds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and a bundled 1890 GB local disk.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine with dedicated processors and local storage designed for high-throughput compression and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor
64.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 1890 GB local disk
Top-tier performance in single-threaded compression and small-model LLM inference
Hardware-accelerated vector and matrix operations via AVX-512 and AMX
Optimized for web serving, batch processing, and compute-heavy workloads
